title: No bounds check for Echo::from_bytes data access
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:749

In handle_icmpv6(), after parsing Echo header:
```rust
let echo = Icmpv6Echo::from_bytes(body)?;
let data = &body[4..]; // After id+seq
```

The from_bytes() at line 492-500 only validates that body has at least 4 bytes:
```rust
if buf.len() < 4 {
    return Err(TooShort::new(4, buf.len()).into());
}
```

Then line 749 accesses body[4..], which is safe (empty slice if exactly 4 bytes).

However, the comment says 'After id+seq' which is misleading - the Echo header parsed by from_bytes is id+seq (4 bytes total), so body[4..] is the echo data, not 'after id+seq'.

Wait, let me re-read. from_bytes receives 'body' which is:
- body = icmpv6_payload[ICMPV6_HEADER_LEN..] = icmpv6_payload[4..]
- So body already has type/code/checksum stripped
- body[0..4] = id (2) + seq (2)  
- body[4..] = echo data

So 'After id+seq' is correct. This is not a bug.

Let me cancel this bead.
close_reason: False positive - body[4..] is always valid after from_bytes succeeds. The slice produces an empty slice if body.len() == 4, which is fine.
